📰 SOLANA NEWS:

- SoFi Enables Direct Solana Deposits

SoFi has become the first U.S. bank to enable direct deposits on the Solana network.

Allowing its ~13.7 million customers to transfer SOL from external wallets directly into their SoFi crypto accounts.

- Solana Domains Now in Jupiter Wallet

.sol domains are now supported in Jupiter Mobile and its wallet browser extension. You can now send assets by simply typing your .sol domain instead of long addresses.

- SolanaCDN Launch

Pipe Network  launched SolanaCDN

A free, open-source validator client enhancement that integrates a global content delivery layer.

Since early Feb, I've been leading product for the @SolanaFndn.

Solana Foundation Product is a brand new team of world-class engineers and PMs that are building full stack, first party experiences & APIs for Solana.

Our primary user is a large enterprise or fintech, but we're also shipping new experiences for everyday users.

Everything we build is AI-ready, designed for LLMs and agents to consume and use.

I'm using @OpenClaw for almost entirely work-related use cases at this point and I was thinking about why. 

I realized an entire industry needs to change and the first companies to do it will have a massive advantage. 

Two factors make personal-use cases much more difficult to implement:

1. Browser-use is extremely brittle. Publishers and website owners do not want automated systems using their websites. Publishers see it as scraping. They actively prevent automated usage. 
2. Many consumer targeted apps/websites do not offer APIs. Why would they? Consumers generally don't need that. 

This combination makes it very difficult to achieve personal-automation use cases since most of them require navigating websites. 

Some example:
> Buy something for me on Amazon
> Renew my car registration on the DMV
> Get me tickets to the movie on Friday

Publishers need to build for an agentic future. The first ones to do so will win.

I’m noticing a lot of drama and noise lately, which is a classic consequence of the bear market. I’ve been spending less and less time here.

I’m just making sure I push my daily content and then I mostly vanish.

Been spending way more time with friends and family and I fucking love it. Real life feels great. Quiet feels good. Building in peace feels even better.

I’m not replying as much, and I’m barely consuming any X content, but I see you. I see who’s still here, still building, still showing up.

I’m here with you guys and always will be. I just want to limit my X intake because a lot of people here move from ignorance and react emotionally, which turns the timeline toxic 24/7 without much self-awareness.

As much as I love crypto and I’m grateful for X, this app can be poison for the mind. I don’t even blame X for it. It’s mostly just humans being human, projecting stress and boredom onto a timeline.

I’m here because I care about my loyal supporters. Everything else is irrelevant. The noise, the drama, the random takes, none of that matters.

I can’t afford to consume garbage content during one of the greatest opportunities of our lifetime. I’d rather spend that time reflecting, thinking clearly, and actually living.

Still here. Just quieter and less distracted.

idOS is about reusable KYC inside stablecoin-native finance.

A very conservative scenario looks like this:

• 1 million users,
• 4 identity reuses per year,
• $5 per reuse.

That’s roughly $20M in transaction volume and about $5M in revenue.

Wow...

Magic Eden is closing products that are not making money

They are:

• Closing EVM marketplaces
• Closing the Bitcoin marketplace
• Stopping NFT buybacks
• Shutting down the Magic Eden wallet

They are focusing on:

• Solana (their main market)
• @DiceyHQ (crypto casino / sportsbook, still in beta)

Why?

80% of their costs were coming from products that made less than 20% of revenue

$ME token stays important:

• 15% of revenue goes to the $ME ecosystem
• 7.5% for buybacks
• 7.5% for USDC rewards to stakers

Goal: make the company simpler, more profitable, and stronger long term

Which I think makes total sense, and what is your thoughts on this changes?

(Partnered with Magic Eden)

RT @cryptokitty3x: It honestly hurts to hear this.

I do not support theft in any form. Information, property, money… taking from others is…

BREAKING 🚨 Magic Eden is going back to its roots (Solana) and focusing more on their profitable products:

- The Solana NFT marketplace (where it all started and where the NFT volume remains)

- Entertainment, which is the next big growth push (Packs, crypto casino/sportsbook)

ME token will stay at the center and profits from these areas will still go towards ME.

Due to unprofitability or inactivity, Magic Eden is sunsetting these products:

- EVM marketplaces
- Bitcoin marketplace (Runes & Ordinals) 
- Bitcoin API
- Magic Eden Wallet 
- NFT Buybacks

From the 24M in revenue generated in 2025, the large majority came from Solana nfts and the entertainment sector, so now they will double down on what works.

🚨 BREAKING

Magic Eden is shutting down all EVM marketplaces (+ wallet) to refocus on Solana and Dicey

With ~50–70% of ME’s revenue coming from Solana the move actually makes a lot of sense

Big W for Solana NFTs as well https://t.co/bhwKNUS5Im
